title: Multi-factor classical convergence as a predictive signal above single-school baseline in Jyotish

hypotheses.description: Multi-factor classical convergence — simultaneous agreement of Parashari house scoring, KP sublord analysis, and Jaimini karakamsha combined with Layer II structural activation (dasha capacity x transit delivery) — predicts life outcome domains at a statistically validated rate above any single school's baseline, as measured by Brier score improvement and signal isolation above user prior probability.
hypotheses.notes: Primary hypothesis — must be tested first; secondary hypotheses only if H1 shows signal

hypotheses.description: Individual rules that survive BH FDR correction at q<0.05 in the training set.
hypotheses.notes: Category A: rules with positive predictive validity

hypotheses.description: Classical rules with significant negative predictive validity — fire when outcome does NOT occur more than expected by chance.
hypotheses.notes: Category C: rules that act as contraindications

hypotheses.description: Layer I x Layer II interaction predicts better than either layer alone.
hypotheses.notes: Interaction hypothesis — tests whether structural activation adds information above classical concordance

cv_strategy.description: Pre-2010 train, 2010+ test — no look-ahead leakage. Events confirmed after 2010 are held out entirely until final test run.

stopping_rule: No analysis is run before 1,000 confirmed training-eligible events exist, regardless of project schedule. This number may only be revised upward.

feature_definitions.convergence_score: Mean of (parashari_score + kp_score + jaimini_score) normalized to [-1,1] per house
feature_definitions.layer2_activation: dasha_capacity_score x transit_delivery_score — both must be positive for activation
feature_definitions.dasha_capacity: Promise engine capacity check: vimshottari MD/AD lord strength in D1 and D9
feature_definitions.transit_delivery: Gochar delivery gate: current transit lords activating natal promise

planned_analyses: 1. Descriptive statistics on feature distributions (no inference)
2. Test H1: convergence_score Brier improvement vs. single-school baselines
3. Test H2: per-rule BH-FDR correction across R01-R23
4. Test H3: negative predictive validity scan
5. Test H4: Layer I x Layer II interaction term
6. Robustness: time-split cross-validation on held-out 2010+ data
